prompt: Implement a PharmaProcure Compliance Procurement Portal for offline B2B purchasing, document control, and evidence-backed operations in a pharmacy distribution environment. Users include a Buyer (clinic or facility procurement staff), Fulfillment Clerk (warehouse pick/pack/ship), Quality Reviewer (compliance and document approval), Finance (records payment status), and System Administrator (configuration and access control). The Angular web interface provides a guided order workspace where a Buyer creates an order from an internal product catalog, submits it for review, and receives immediate, on-screen status feedback for each step in the lifecycle (created, under review, approved, payment recorded, pick/pack, shipped, received, returned, canceled). Orders support partial shipment and partial receipt, with clear discrepancy prompts when quantities differ, and exceptions such as damaged goods or temperature excursion captured as after-sales cases tied to the original order for end-to-end traceability. The same UI includes an official document center for controlled templates (e.g., SOPs, packing slips, certificates), automatic numbering (prefix by doc type and year with a 6-digit sequence, resetting each 01/01), drafting and routed approval, and final archiving with online preview and dynamic watermarking that shows username, timestamp, and document number. Field staff can create one-tap check-in records from a lightweight screen, attach photos, short audio notes, and text comments, and automatically stamp the record with local device time and, if the browser provides it, latitude/longitude without displaying maps; any edits are clearly highlighted with a full revision trail.The backend uses Spring Boot to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the Angular client over a local network, keeping the system fully functional without internet connectivity and storing all data in PostgreSQL for on-prem persistence. Core entities include orders with a configurable state machine, shipments/receipts for partial fulfillment, returns with reason codes, documents with template-derived numbering and metadata tags, check-ins with attachment references and immutable hashes, and audit events for every create/update/approve/download action. Security and compliance are enforced through RBAC for page and API access plus fine-grained authorization down to action-level and data-scope rules, with dual approval required for critical actions such as document destruction, retention overrides, and order cancellation after approval; dual approval must be completed by two different users within 24 hours or the request expires. Passwords are stored using a modern one-way hash (e.g., BCrypt) with a minimum length of 12 characters and at least three character classes, failed login lockout after 5 attempts for 15 minutes, and an offline CAPTCHA challenge triggered after 3 failures. The API layer applies per-user rate limiting (e.g., 60 requests/minute) and input validation to mitigate abuse, with CSRF protection for browser sessions and systematic defenses against XSS and SQL injection. File uploads are constrained to approved types (PDF/JPG/PNG/WAV) and size (25 MB max), validated by MIME and file signature checks, and fingerprinted using SHA-256 to detect tampering; check-in evidence and archived documents store hashes and a server-side signature using a locally managed key. Logs are desensitized by masking sensitive values (e.g., showing only the last 4 characters of identifiers), and personal data collection is minimized to what is operationally required for procurement, approvals, and auditing.
